2606.10933.title: Frontier Coding Agents Use Metaprogramming to Adapt to Unfamiliar Programming Languages
2606.10933.paper_summary: LLM-based coding agents are usually evaluated in familiar software settings: mainstream languages, common libraries, and public repositories. These benchmarks remain important, but they can hide how agents behave when the language itself is unfamiliar. We evaluate six contemporary coding agents on four esoteric programming languages using a sequential setup with file editing, local execution, and hidden-test grading. Our protocol exposes capability differences between these agents that mainstream coding and agentic benchmarks such as SWE-Bench Verified and Terminal-Bench 2.0 compress into much narrower bands. We observe that the strongest agents, Claude Opus 4.6 and GPT-5.4 xhigh, often avoid writing the target language directly. On Brainfuck and Befunge-98, they write Python programs that generate target-language code and debug those generators locally. Forbidding this metaprogramming strategy causes large performance drops. Text guidance distilled from this strategy does not materially improve weaker agents. In contrast, Opus-derived Python helper code for building generators, with no solved benchmark programs or hidden-test answers, sharply improves Sonnet 4.6 and GPT-5.4 mini on the same problems, while Haiku 4.5 remains low. More interpreter calls and output tokens improve stronger agents but leave weaker agents near their original performance, indicating that these resources amplify useful strategies rather than create them. Together, these results show that strong coding agents adapt to unfamiliar languages by using tools, feedback, and workspace state to build a working model of the target language. Metaprogramming is the clearest case, but the broader gap is constructing and debugging a strategy that works under the target language's rules.
2606.10933.paper_summary_zh: LLM 基礎的編碼代理通常在熟悉的軟體環境中進行評估：主流語言、常見庫和公共存儲庫。這些基準仍然很重要，但它們可能隱藏了代理在語言本身不熟悉時的行為。我們在四種冷門編程語言上評估了六個當代編碼代理，使用了包含文件編輯、本地執行和隱藏測試評分的序列設置。我們的協議揭示了這些代理之間的能力差異，而主流編碼和代理基準如 SWE-Bench Verified 和 Terminal-Bench 2.0 則將其壓縮到更狹窄的範圍。我們觀察到，最強的代理，Claude Opus 4.6 和 GPT-5.4 xhigh，通常避免直接編寫目標語言。在 Brainfuck 和 Befunge-98 上，它們編寫 Python 程式來生成目標語言代碼，並在本地調試這些生成器。禁止這種元編程策略會導致性能大幅下降。從這一策略提煉出的文本指導並未實質性改善較弱的代理。相比之下，來自 Opus 的 Python 幫助代碼用於構建生成器，沒有已解決的基準程式或隱藏測試答案，卻在相同問題上顯著改善了 Sonnet 4.6 和 GPT-5.4 mini，而 Haiku 4.5 仍然表現不佳。更多的解釋器調用和輸出標記改善了較強的代理，但對較弱的代理幾乎沒有影響，這表明這些資源放大了有用的策略，而不是創造它們。綜合這些結果顯示，強大的編碼代理通過使用工具、反饋和工作區狀態來適應不熟悉的語言，以建立目標語言的工作模型。元編程是最明顯的案例，但更廣泛的差距在於構建和調試在目標語言規則下可行的策略。
2606.10933.author: Aman Sharma et.al.
2606.10933.authors: Aman Sharma, Sushrut Thorat, Paras Chopra
